questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass m is sliding down an inclined plane with constant speed. At a certain instant t0, its height above the ground is h. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the block and the the plane is μ. If the block reaches the ground at a later instant tg, then the energy dissipated by friction in the time interval tg-t0 is,
questions.solution:
Let u be the constant speed of the block.

According to the work-energy theorem, the change in kinetic energy will be equal to the work done by the forces acting on the block in sliding down.

Wnet=∆K.E

Here, ∆K.E=12mu2=0 as the speed is constant.

Thus, Wf+Wmg=0, where Wf and Wmg are the work done by frictional and gravitational forces.

Wf=-Wmg=-mgh, here g is the acceleration due to gravity and h is height.

Hence, energy dissipated by friction is mgh.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle of mass <math><mi mathvariant="bold">m</mi></math> is moving in a circular path of constant radius <math><mi mathvariant="bold">r</mi></math> such that its centripetal acceleration <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="bold">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="bold">c</mi></mrow></msub></math> is varying with time <math><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></math> as <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="bold">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="bold">c</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> , where <math><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></math> is a constant. The power delivered to the particle by the force acting on it is
questions.solution:
ac=k2 rt2

or v2r= k2 rt2

or v=krt

Therefore, tangential acceleration, at =dvdt= kr

or Tangential force,

Ft=mat=mkr

Only tangential force does work.

Power=Ft v=mkrkrt

or Power=mk2 r2 t
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When a steel wire fixed at one end is pulled by a constant force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>F</mi></math> at its other end, its length increases by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math>. Which of the following statements is not correct?
options: Work done by the external force is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>F</mi><mi>l</mi></math>.
Some heat is produced in the wire in the process.
The elastic potential energy of the wire is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>F</mi><mi>l</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>.
The heat produced is equal to half of the elastic potential energy stored in the wire.
questions.solution:
Given that

Elongation in wire=l

Let Young' modulus of wire =Y

Let Length of wire =L 

Let area of wire =A

Applied force =F 

Elastic wire behaves as a spring whose force constant is equal to k=YAL

Work done by spring is given by,

W=12kx2

∴  F=kx

⇒  W=12Fx

∴  x=l

⇒ W=12Fl

Work done by applied force

Wforce=force ×displacement

⇒ Wforce=Fl

Potential energy stored in wire

U=12kx2

∴  F=kx

⇒  U=12Fx

∴  x=l

⇒  U=12Fl

The heat produced is equal to the elastic potential energy stored in the wire.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A motor of power $P_0$ is used to deliver water at a certain rate through a given horizontal pipe. To increase the rate of flow of water through the same pipe $n$ times, the power of the motor is increased to $P_1$. The ratio of $P_1$ to $P_0$ is
questions.solution:
Power of motor initially $=P_0$ Let, rate of flow of motor $=(x)$ $\text { Since, power, } \begin{aligned} P_0 & =\frac{\text { work }}{\text { time }}=\frac{m g y}{t} \\ & =m g\left(\frac{y}{t}\right), \\ \frac{y}{t} & =x=\text { rate of flow of water } \end{aligned}$
If rate of flow of water is increased by $n$ times, $i e$, (nx). Increased power $\begin{aligned} P_1 & =\frac{m g y^{\prime}}{t} \\ & =m g\left(\frac{y^{\prime}}{t}\right)=m g n \cdot x \end{aligned}$
The ratio of power $\begin{gathered} \frac{P_1}{P_0}=\frac{n m g x}{m g x} \\ \frac{P_1}{P_0}=\frac{n}{1} \Rightarrow P_1: P_0=n: 1 \end{gathered}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The initial configuration of the system is as shown in the figure.



The string and the pulley are light. The bob is released and the string starts wrapping around the pulley. (The pulley is held in place by a force applied at the centre)



Find the rate r at which the length of the string wrapped around the pulley increases (as a function of θ).
questions.solution:
At any instant of time, the length of the string wrapped on the pulley is Rθ and the remaining length L=l-Rθ

The rate r=Rdθdt=Rω=Rvl-Rθ, where v is the speed of the particle

The height through which the particle has come down

h=R1-cosθ+l-Rθsinθ

⇒v=2gR1-cosθ+l-Rθsinθ

So,

r=Rl-Rθ2gl-Rθsinθ+R1-cosθ
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the arrangement shown in the figure, work done by the string on the block of mass \(0.36 \mathrm{~kg}\) during the first second after the blocks are released from state of rest is (Ignore friction and mass of the string.) (Acceleration due to gravity, \(g\) \(=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\))
questions.solution:
According to the question,
Given, mass of block \(1, m_1=0.36 \mathrm{~kg}\) mass of block \(2, m_2=0.72 \mathrm{~kg}\) and acceleration due to gravity, \(g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\) Now, Dynamic equation of block \(m_1\left(m_2 > m_1\right)\). \(\therefore \quad T-m_1 g=m_1 a\)...(i) Dynamic equation of block \(m_2\left(m_2 > m_1\right)\). \(\therefore \quad m_2 g-T=m_2 a \quad \ldots (ii)\) Thus, from adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get \(\therefore \quad T-m_1 g+m_2 g-T=m_1 a+m_2 a\) \(m_2 g-m_1 g=a\left(m_1+m_2\right)\) or \(a=\frac{\left(m_2-m_1\right) g}{\left(m_1+m_2\right)}\) Hence, acceleration, \(a=\left(\frac{m_2-m_1}{m_1+m_2}\right) g\) \(\therefore\) Acceleration, \(a=\frac{(0.72-0.36)}{1.08} \times 10\) \(a=\frac{3.6}{1.08} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 \quad \ldots (iii)\) Now, the tension force in the string is, for block 1st, \(m_1=0.36\) \(\begin{aligned} & T-m_1 g=m_1 a \\ & \frac{T-m_1 g}{m_1}=a \\ & \frac{T-3.6}{0.36}=\frac{3.6}{1.08} \\ & T=3.6+0.36 \times \frac{3.6}{1.08} \\ & T=4.8 \quad \ldots (iv) \end{aligned}\) Distance travelled by mass \(m_1\) block, By displacement-time relation, Displacement, \(s=u t+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{at}^2\) \([\because u=0]\) [by kinetic equation for uniformly motion] \(s=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{3.6}{1.08} \times(1)^2 \quad[\therefore t=1\) second given] \(s=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{3.6}{1.08}\)...(v) Now, work done by the string on the block of mass \(\left(m_1\right)\) is \(0.36 \mathrm{~kg}\). \(\therefore\) Work done, \(W=\) Tension force \((T) \times\) displacement \((s)\) \(W=T \times \frac{1}{2} a t^2\) From Eqs. (iv) and (v), we get \(\begin{aligned} & W=4.8 \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{3.6}{1.08} \\ & W=\frac{16}{2} \Rightarrow W=8 \mathrm{~J} \end{aligned}\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A car of mass $1000 \mathrm{~kg}$ having a velocity of $10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ collides a horizontally mounted spring. If the spring constant is $4000 \mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$, then the maximum compression of the spring is
questions.solution:
Mass of car, $m=1000 \mathrm{~kg}$
Velocity, $v=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
Spring constant, $\mathrm{k}=4000 \mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$
Apply the conservation of energy
$\frac{1}{2} m v^2=\frac{1}{2} k(\Delta x)^2$
$\Delta \mathrm{x}=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{k}}}=\sqrt{\frac{1000 \times 100}{4000}}$
Compression of the spring $\Delta x=5 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass M slides on a frictionless surface with an initial speed of v0. On top of the block, there is a small block of mass m. The coefficient of friction between the blocks is μ. The system of blocks encounters an ideal spring with force constant k. The maximum value of k for which the blocks don't slip relative to each other is
questions.solution:
12M+mv02=12kx2

x=v0M+mk

Also for the upper block

fmax=μmg=mkm+Mx

μg=km+Mv0m+Mk

⇒km+M=μgv02

⇒k=μgv02m+M
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A cannon shell fired breaks into two equal parts at its highest point. One part retraces the path to the cannon with kinetic energy $E_1$ and kinetic energy of the second part is $E_2$, relation between $E_1$ and $E_2$ is
questions.solution:
At highest point
$m u \cos \theta=-\frac{m}{2} u \cos \theta+\frac{m}{2} v$
$m u \cos \theta+\frac{m}{2} u \cos \theta=\frac{m}{2} v$
$\frac{2 m u \cos \theta+m u \cos \theta}{2}=\frac{m}{2} v$
$$
\begin{aligned}
m v & =3 m u \cos \theta \\
v & =3 u \cos \theta
\end{aligned}
$$
The kinetic energy
$$
\begin{aligned}
& E_1=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{m}{2} u^2 \cos ^2 \theta \\
& E_1=\frac{1}{4} m u^2 \cos ^2 \theta
\end{aligned}
$$
Similarly,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& E_2=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{m}{2} \times 9 u^2 \cos ^2 \theta \\
& E_2=\frac{9}{4} m u^2 \cos ^2 \theta
\end{aligned}
$$
The relation between the $E_1$ and $E_2$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& E_2=9 \times E_1 \\
& E_2=9 E_1
\end{aligned}
$$
(from the Eq. (ii))
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The velocity $v$ reached by a car of mass $m$ at certain distance from the starting point driven with constant power $P$ is such that
questions.solution: $P=\frac{\frac{1}{2} m v^2}{t} \Rightarrow v^2=\frac{2 P t}{m} \Rightarrow v^2 \propto \frac{2 P}{m}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ box placed at the origin starts sliding along $\mathrm{x}$-axis under the action of a force $\vec{F}=F \hat{i}$. Its acceleration as a function of $x$ is given by $a(x)=\beta . x$ where $\beta=5 \mathrm{~s}^{-2}$. The work done by $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}$ in moving the box from $\mathrm{x}=2 \mathrm{~cm}$ to $\mathrm{x}=5 \mathrm{~cm}$ in joule is
questions.solution:
Work done $=\int \mathrm{Fdx}=\int_{0.02}^{0.05} \mathrm{~m}(\beta \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1 \times 5}{2} \times\left[\mathrm{x}^2\right]_{0.02}^{0.05} \\ & =\frac{5}{2}\left[25 \times 10^{-4}-4 \times 10^{-4}\right] \\ & =\frac{5}{2} \times 21 \times 10^{-4} \\ & =52.5 \times 10^{-4} \text { Joule. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: v-t graph of an object of mass 1 kg is shown. Select the Wrong statement.
options: Work done on the object in 30 s is zero
The average acceleration of the object is zero
The average velocity of the object is zero
The average force on the object is zero
questions.solution:
Initial velocity = final velocity = 0.

As area under v-t graph is displacement

displacement ≠ 0

therefore average velocity is not zero
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass $m=25 \mathrm{~kg}$ sliding on a smooth horizontal surface with a velocity $v=3 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ meets the spring of spring constant $k=100 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$ fixed at one end as shown in figure. The maximum compression of the spring and velocity of block as it returns to the original position respectively are
questions.solution:
When block strikes the spring, the kinetic energy of block converts into potential energy of spring ie,
$\frac{1}{2} m v^2=\frac{1}{2} k x^2$
where $x$ is compression in spring.
$x=\sqrt{\frac{m v^2}{k}}$
$=\sqrt{\frac{25 \times(3)^2}{100}}=\sqrt{\frac{9}{4}}=\frac{3}{2}=1.5 \mathrm{~m}$
When block returns to the original position, again potential energy converts into kinetic energy of the block, so velocity of the block is same as before but its sign changes as it goes to mean position.
Hence, $\quad v=-3 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solid spherical ball is rolled up an inclined plane of angle of inclination $30^{\circ}$ with an initial speed of $4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at the bottom of the inclination. How far will the ball go up the plane. $\left(\right.$ Use $\left.g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { As P.E } \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{i}}+(\mathrm{K} . \mathrm{E})_{\mathrm{i}}=(\mathrm{P} . \mathrm{E})_{\mathrm{f}}+(\mathrm{K} . \mathrm{E})_{\mathrm{f}} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 0+\frac{1}{2} \times \mathrm{m} \times 4^2+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{5} \mathrm{mR}^2\right) \times \frac{4^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}=\mathrm{mgh} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{4^2}{2}+\frac{1}{5} \times 4^2=10 \mathrm{~h} \Rightarrow 8+3.2=10 \mathrm{~h} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 1.12=\mathrm{h} \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=1.12 \mathrm{~m} \\ & \text { So, } \ell=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{\sin 30^{\circ}}=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{1 / 2}=2 \mathrm{~h}=2.24 \mathrm{~m}=224 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' and charge ' $\mathrm{q}$ ' is connected to a point ' $O$ ' with an inextensible string. This system is on a horizontal table. An electric field (E) is applied perpendicular to the string and in the plane of the horizontal table. The tension in the string when it becomes parallel to the electric field is
questions.solution:
Let the length of the string is L Using the work energy theorem $F \cdot L=\frac{1}{2} m v^2-0$
$q E \cdot L=\frac{1}{2} m v^2 \Rightarrow v^2=\frac{2 q E L}{m}$
If $\mathrm{T}$ is the tension in the string.
When it becomes parallel to the electric field.
$\begin{aligned}
& T-q E=\frac{m v^2}{L} \\
& T=q E+\frac{m v^2}{L} \\
& =q E+\frac{m}{L} \times \frac{2 q E L}{M} ; T=3 q E
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A frictionless track ABCDE ends in a circular loop of radius R, from the given figure. A body slides down the track from point A which is at a height h=5 cm. The maximum value of R for the body to successfully complete the loop is
questions.solution:
H is sliding on a smooth plane, now using conservation of energy at the highest point A, there is potential energy mgH.
Now, at a point C the normal force is,
 N=mg+v2R 

Here, due to circular motion, a centripetal force is also present, so according to the law of conservation of energy,

 N=mg+v2R=0v=gR...i
Now, according to the law of conservation of energy,
mgH=mg2R+12mv2mgH=mg2R+12mgRH=2R+12RH=5R2=5R=2 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A stone is projected vertically upwards with speed ' $v$ '. Another stone of same mass is projected at an angle of $60^{\circ}$ with the vertical with the same speed ' $v$ '. The ratio of their potential energies at the highest points of their journey is $\left[\sin 30^{\circ}=\cos 60^{\circ}=0.5\right.$, $\left.\cos 30^{\circ}=\sin 60^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right]$
questions.solution:
P.E. of the stone projected vertically is, 
P.E. = mgh
But $\mathrm{h}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^2}{2 \mathrm{~g}}$
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad \text { P. } \mathrm{E}_1 & =\mathrm{mg}\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}^2}{2 \mathrm{~g}}\right) \\
& =\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{2}... (i)
\end{aligned}$
For the second stone thrown at an angle $\theta$ to the horizontal,
$\begin{array}{ll} 
& \mathrm{h}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^2 \sin ^2 \theta}{2 \mathrm{~g}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^2 \sin ^2 30^{\circ}}{2 \mathrm{~g}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^2}{8 \mathrm{~g}} \\
\therefore \quad & \text { P.E. } 2=\mathrm{mg}\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}^2}{8 \mathrm{~g}}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{8}... (ii)
\end{array}$
Dividing equation (i) by equation (ii) -
$-\frac{P \cdot E_1}{P \cdot E_2}=\frac{\left(\frac{m v^2}{2}\right)}{\left(\frac{m v^2}{8}\right)}=4: 1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body slides down a smooth inclined plane having angle ' $\theta$ ' and reaches the bottom with velocity 'v'. If a body is a sphere then its linear velocity at the bottom of the plane is
questions.solution:
The linear velocity of the body, $v=\sqrt{2 g h}$
The velocity of the sphere about its centre,
$v_{C M}=\sqrt{\frac{2 g h}{1+\frac{K^{2}}{R^{2}}}}=\frac{v}{\sqrt{1+\frac{K^{2}}{R^{2}}}} \ldots(\text { i })$
For uniform solid sphere,
$\frac{K^{2}}{R^{2}}=\frac{2}{5}$
Substituting value in Eq. (i), we get
$v_{C M}=\frac{v}{\sqrt{1+\left(\frac{2}{5}\right)}}=\sqrt{\frac{5}{7} v}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball suspended by a thread, swings in a vertical plane so that its acceleration in the extreme position and lowest position are equal in magnitude. Angle <math><mi mathvariant="italic">θ</mi></math> of thread deflection in the extreme position will be:
questions.solution:
aA=g sinθ  (only tangential)

aB=v2l (only radial)

K.E. + P.E. = 12m02+mgl 1-cos⁡θ=12mv2

v2=2gl 1-cos⁡θ ...........(i)

∴aB=v2l=2g (1-cosθ)

Since, aA=aB

∴g sinθ=2g 1-cosθ⟹2sin⁡θ2 cos⁡θ2=2 ×2sin2⁡θ2

⟹tan⁡θ2=12  ⟹ θ=2tan-1⁡12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A bullet of mass $10 \mathrm{~g}$ is fired horizontally with a velocity $1000 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ from a rifle situated at a height $50 \mathrm{~m}$ above the ground. If the bullet reaches the ground with a velocity $500 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$, the work done against air resistance in the trajectory of the bullet is : $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.solution:
From equation of motion,
$v^2=u^2-2 a s$
$(500)^2=(1000)^2-2 \times a \times s$
$s=\frac{(1000)^2-(500)^2}{2 a}=\frac{375000}{a}$
$\therefore$ Work done against air resistance $=F S$
$=m a \times s$
$=\frac{10}{1000} a \times \frac{375000}{a}$
$=3750 \mathrm{~J}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For the shown situation, if a collision between blocks A and B is perfectly elastic, then find the maximum energy (in joules) stored in the spring.
questions.solution:
According to the conservation of linear momentum, 

Pi→ = Pf→,

where Pi is the initial momentum of the system and Pf is the momentum after collision.
 

Spring has maximum energy at the time of maximum compression. 
And at maximum compression, velocity of both the blocks should be equal.
Applying momentum conservation,

Pi = Pf 
3×2=(3+6)×V
where, V is the velocity of both the blocks after collision. 
V=23 m s-1

Now, applying conservation of energy, 
Change in kinetic energy = Potential energy gained 
∆KE=12Kx2=Usp
⇒12×3×(2)2-12(3)232+12(6)232=Umax.

On solving, 
Umax=4 J.
Here, maximum energy stored in the spring is 4 J.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A 0.5 kg block slides from A on horizontal track with an initial speed of 3 m s–1 towards a weightless horizontal spring of length 1 m and force constant 2 N m–1. The part AB of the track is frictionless and the part BC has the coefficient of static and kinetic friction as 0.22 and 0.20 respectively. If the distance AB and BD are 2 m and 2.14 m respectively, the total distance through which the block moves before it comes to rest completely is -
questions.solution:
At D, KE=12 mv2 – μkmg BD

= 12 × 0.5 × 32 – 0.2 × 0.5 × 10 × 2.14

= 2.25 – 2.14=0.11 J

0.11=12 kx2 + μRmgx⇒x2+x–0.11=0

⇒x=0.1 m

Total distance covered=2 + 2.14 + 0.1 =4.24 m
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body of mass ' $\mathrm{m}$ ' attached at the end of a string is just completing the loop in a vertical circle. The apparent weight of the body at the lowest point in its path is ( $\mathrm{g}=$ gravitational acceleration)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A spherical ball of mass m is kept at the highest point in the space between two fixed, concentric spheres A and B (see fig.). The smaller sphere A has a radius R and space between the two spheres has a width d. The ball has a diameter very slightly less than d. All surfaces are frictionless. The ball is given a gentle push (towards the right in the figure). The angle made by the radius vector of the ball with the upward vertical is denoted by θ.  What is the total normal reaction force exerted by the spheres on the ball in terms of angle θ?
options: 2mg ( 3cosθ – 1 )
questions.solution:
h=R+d21-cos θ
 

 
The velocity of the ball at an angle θ is

ν2=2gh=2R+d21-cos θg  ...... (i)

Let N be the normal reaction (away from the centre) at angle θ. Then, mg cos θ-N=mν2R+d2

Substituting the value of ν 2 from equation (i), we get

mg cos θ-N=2mg1-cos θ

N=mg3 cos θ-2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A uniform chain of length $l$ and mass $m$ lies on the surface of a smooth hemisphere of radius $R(R>l)$ with one end tied to the top the hemisphere as shown in the figure.
Gravitational potential energy of the chain with respect to the base of the hemisphere is
questions.solution: We have, $\quad \frac{h}{R}=\sin \theta$ or $h=R \sin \theta$ Also, $d l=R d \theta$ Mass of $d l$ length of chain $=d m=\frac{m}{l} \cdot d l$ PE of $d m$ mass $$ =d U=d m g h=\frac{m g h}{l} \cdot d l=\frac{m g h}{l} R d \theta=\frac{m g R^2}{l} \sin \theta d \theta $$ So, PE of complete chain is $$ U=\int_{\pi / 2}^{\pi / 2-\theta} d U=\frac{m g R^2}{l} \sin \left(\frac{l}{R}\right) $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A rifle bullet loses <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>25</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mrow><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></math> of its velocity in passing through a plank. The least number of such planks required just to stop the bullet is
questions.solution:
If u is the initial velocity of bullet then after passing through one plank the velocity became v=u-u25=24u25

Using kinematic equation for uniform acceleration motion, v2-u2=2as

24u252-u2=2as⇒2as=u2-625+576625⇒2as=-u249625

If n number of planks are required to bring the bullet at rest then, v=0

⇒0-u2=2ans⇒-u2=n-49u2625⇒n=62549=12.75 or 13

Thus, 13 planks will be required in order to bring the bullet at rest.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A sphere is suspended by a thread of length $l$ What minimum horizontal velocity has to be imparted the ball for it to reach the height of the suspension?
questions.solution:
KE given to a sphere at lowest point
= potential energy at the height of suspension $\frac{1}{2} m v^{2}=m g l$
$\therefore v=\sqrt{2 g l}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A cyclist comes to a skidding stop in \(10 \mathrm{~m}\). During this process, the force on the cycle due to the road is \(200 \mathrm{~N}\) and is directly opposite to the motion. How much work does the road do on the cycle?
questions.solution:
Displacement travelled by cyclist to reach skidding stop, \(s=10 \mathrm{~m}\)
Force on the cyclist due to road,
\(F=200 \mathrm{~N}\)
Since, force is applied directly opposite to motion (or displacement), hence \(\theta=180^{\circ}\)
\(\therefore\) Work done by the road on the cycle
\(W=F s \cos \theta=200 \times 10 \times \cos 180^{\circ}=-2000 \mathrm{~J}\)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the diagram shown, the blocks A and B are of the same mass M and the mass of the block C is M1. Friction is present only under the block A. The whole system is suddenly released from the state of rest. The minimum coefficient of friction to keep the block A in the state of rest is equal to
options: None of these
questions.solution:
When extension in spring is maximum, then maximum spring force will act on block A. In this situation required (calculated ) friction to keep block A at rest will be minimum
Let x is maximum extension and at maximum extension kinetic energy of whole system is zero.
By COME
Gain in E.P.E. of spring = Loss in  G.P.E. of block C

So, 12kx2=M1gx

x=2M1gk  ......................(i)

To keep block A at rest

kx≤μN

kx≤μMg

By putting value of x from equation (i)

2M1g≤μMg

μ≥2M1M

So    μmin=2M1M
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0.1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi>kg</mi></math> is connected to a spring of unknown spring constant k. It is compressed to a distance <math><mi>x</mi> </math> from its equilibrium position and released from rest. After approaching half the distance <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> from the equilibrium position, it hits another block and comes to rest momentarily, while the other block moves with velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi> </mi><mn>3</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The total initial energy of the spring is:
questions.solution:
By mechanical energy conservation between compression positions x and x2

12kx2=12kx22+12mv2

12kx2-12kx24=12mv2

12kx234=12mv2

v=3kx24m=3kmx2

On collision with a block at rest

∵ Velocities are exchanged ⇒ elastic collision between identical masses.

∴v=3=3kmx2

⇒6=3km  x

⇒x=6m3k

∴ The initial energy of the spring is

U=12k x2=12k×36m3k=6m

U=6×0.1=0.6 J
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ball of mass $2 \mathrm{~g}$ released from the top of an inclined plane describes a circular motion of radius $20 \mathrm{~cm}$ in the vertical plane upon reaching the bottom. The minimum height of the inclined plane is
questions.solution:
The motion of ball is as shown below
At bottom of incline (at $P$ ) the potential energy of ball is converted into kinetic energy i.e., $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} m v^2=m g h \\ & \Rightarrow \quad v=\sqrt{2 g h} \\ & \end{aligned}$ To complete the vertical circle, the velocity of ball should be at least $\sqrt{5 g R}$. $\Rightarrow \quad \begin{aligned} v & \geq \sqrt{5 g R} \\ \sqrt{2 g h} & \geq \sqrt{5 g R}\end{aligned}$ [From Eq. (i)] $\Rightarrow \quad h \geq \frac{5}{2} R$ or $h_{\min }=\frac{5}{2} \times 20=50 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If W1, W2 and W3 represent the work done in moving a particle from A to B along three different paths 1, 2 and 3 respectively (as shown) in the gravitational field of a point mass m. Find the correct relation between W1, W2 and W3.
questions.solution: Gravitational field is a conservative force field. In a conservative force field work done is path independent
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A long spring is stretched by $2 \mathrm{~cm}$ and its potential energy is $U$. If the spring is stretched by $10 \mathrm{~cm}$; its potential energy will be
questions.solution:
The potential energy of a stretched spring is
$$
U=\frac{1}{2} k x^{2}
$$
Here, $k=$ spring constant, $x=$ elongation in spring. But given that, the elongation is $2 \mathrm{~cm}$.
So, $U=\frac{1}{2} k(2)^{2}$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad U=\frac{1}{2} k \times 4 ...(i)
$$
If elongation is $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ then potential energy
$$
\begin{aligned}
U^{\prime} &=\frac{1}{2} k(10)^{2} \\
U^{\prime} &=\frac{1}{2} k \times 100 ...(ii)
\end{aligned}
$$
On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i), we have
$$
\frac{U^{\prime}}{U}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} k \times 100}{\frac{1}{2} k \times 4}
$$
or $\frac{U^{\prime}}{U}=25 \Rightarrow U^{\prime}=25 U$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A weightless string can support a tension up to $30 \mathrm{~N}$. A stone of mass $0.5 \mathrm{~kg}$ is tied to its one end and is revolved in a circular path of radius $2 \mathrm{~m}$ in a vertical plane. Then the maximum angular velocity of the stone will be (acceleration due to gravity $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
Considering force balance:
$\begin{aligned} & T_{\max }=m \omega_{\max }^2 r+m g \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{T_{\text {max }}}{m}=\omega_{\max }^2 r+g \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{30 \mathrm{~N}}{0.5 \mathrm{~kg}}-10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2=\omega_{\max }^2 r \\ & \Rightarrow \omega_{\max }=\sqrt{\frac{50}{2}}=5 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: One end of a spring of natural length 2 m and spring constant k = 100 N/m is fixed at the ground and the other is fitted with a smooth ring of mass 1 kg which is allowed to slide on a horizontal rod fixed at a height 2 m (diagram). Initially, the spring makes an angle of 37o with  the vertical when the system is released from rest. Find the speed (in m/s) of the ring when the spring becomes vertical. ( Take sin37o=35 )
questions.solution:
tan  3 7 ∘ = l h ,  3 4 = l h ,  l = 3 h 4

x = extension in spring

          = h 2 + l 2 - h

          = 9 h 2 + 1 6 h 2 1 6 - h = h 4

         1 2 kx 2 = 1 2 mv 2 [By work energy theorem]

⇒     K m h 4 2 = v 2

⇒       v = h 4 k m = 5 m/s
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniform chain of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> and mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> overhangs a horizontal table with its two-third part on the table. The friction coefficient between the table and the chain is <math><mi>μ</mi></math>. The work done by friction during the period, the chain slips of the table is
questions.solution:
The linear mass density is ML. The small work done for the slippage on the small distance dl is given by

dW=-μMLgldl

Now, total work done

W=∫02L3-μMgLl dl

=  -μMgLl2202L3=-μMg2L4L29-0

⇒   W=-29μMgL
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A small body slides down a smooth uneven surface from a height $H$, which eventually emerges into a circular loop of radius $R( < H)$. What should be the value of $H$, so that the force on the body at $A$ is $\sqrt{2}$ times its weight?
questions.solution:
Given, height through which body slides $=H$ Radius of circular loop $=R$ Force on body $A, F_A=\sqrt{2}$ weight $(w)$
Let, velocity of body at $A=v_A$ Acceleration due to gravity $=g$ $$ \frac{m v_A^2}{R}=\sqrt{2} w=\sqrt{2} m g \Rightarrow v_A^2=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{Rg} $$ By using law of conservation of energy Energy at position $(P)=$ Energy at position $(A)$ $$ \begin{array}{rlrl} & & m g H & =\frac{1}{2} m v_A^2+m g R \\ \Rightarrow & 2 g H & =v_A^2+2 g R=\sqrt{2} g R+2 g R=(\sqrt{2}+2) g R \\ \Rightarrow & & H & =\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+1\right) R=1.7 R=1.5 R=\frac{3}{2} R \end{array} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A small positively charged ball of mass m is suspended by an insulating thread of negligible mass. Another positively charged small ball is moved very slowly from a large distance until it is in the original position of the first ball. As a result, the first ball rises by h. How much work has been done?
questions.solution:
mgF=Ix; F=KqQx2

x2;I=h:x



X=kqQ2mg2, Eelectrostatic=kqQx=2mgh

∴W=2mgh+mgh=3mgh
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> accelerates uniformly from rest to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> in time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. The instantaneous power delivered to the body as a function of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Acceleration a = ν 1 t⁡ 1  

∴   Velocity ( ν ) = 0 + a t⁡ = ν 1 t⁡ 1 t⁡ 

Power P⁡ = Force × velocity = m⁡ a ν  

or P⁡ = m⁡ ( ν 1 t⁡ 1 ) × ( ν 1 t⁡ t⁡ 1 ) = m⁡ ν 1 2 t⁡ t⁡ 1 2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A charged particle of mass ‘ m ’ and charge ‘ q ’ moving under the influence of uniform electric field E →i^ and a uniform magnetic field B →k^ follows a trajectory from point P to Q as shown in figure. The velocities at P and Q are respectively, vi→ and -2vj→ . Then which of the following statements (A, B, C, D) are the correct? (Trajectory shown is schematic and not to scale)



A E=32mv2qa
B Rate of work done by the electric field at P is 32mv3a
C Rate of work done by both the fields at Q is zero
D The difference between the magnitude of angular momentum of the particle at P and Q is 2mav .
questions.solution:
(A) by work energy theorem

Wmag+Wele=12m(2v)2-12m(v)2

0+qE02a=32mv2
E0=34mv2qa

(B) Rate of work done at A= power of electric force

=qE0V
=34mv3a

(C) at Q,dwdt=0 for both forces

D ΔL→=(-m2 v 2ak^)-(- mvak )

|ΔL→|=3mva
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
